new_terms.context: A unit used to manage and distribute coolant within a liquid cooling system.
The secondary cooling loop that circulates coolant directly to the compute equipment.
The primary water loop provided by the data center facility to remove heat from the CDU.
A system that uses outdoor air or water to cool the data center without mechanical refrigeration.
The pressure provided by a pump in a hydraulic system, often measured in height or psi.
The temperature of the coolant as it enters the compute tray or cooling system.
The temperature of the coolant as it leaves the compute tray after absorbing heat.
The difference between the inlet and outlet temperatures in a cooling loop.
